LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AGESONG LIVING LLC'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT AS TO UNIVERSITY MOUND OLD LADIES HOME IS GRANTED WITH MODIFICATION. NOTHING IN THE RECORD TO SUGGEST THAT PLAINTIFF, IN FILING THE INSTANT APPLICATION FOR A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT, HAS ANY PURPOSE OTHER THAN THE RECOVERY ON THE CLAIM UPON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS BASED. THUS, THE APPLICATION IS GRANTED FOR THE ISSUANCE OF A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $165,510.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Continued To Nov-12-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JILL B. ROWE OF COOPER WHITE & COOPER, TEL. NO. 433-1900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHON M. SPINELLI OF RMKB, TEL. NO. (650) 780-1644 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF AGESONG LIVING LLC's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AS TO UNIVERSITY MOUND OLD LADIES HOME- Plaintiff's application for a right to attach order and a writ of attachment is granted with modification. First, Plaintiff has shown that the claim is one for which an attachment may be issued since it a claim for money based on an express, unsecured commercial contract-the Management Agreement pursuant to which Plaintiff would manage the senior residential care facility owned and operated by Defendant in exchange for a monthly fee of $15,000. (Dec. of Shabahangi, Ex. A.) Second, the total amount of the alleged breach is readily ascertainable because the Management Agreement expressly provides for the payment of $15,000 per month starting in August 2013, thereby entitling Plaintiff at this point to eleven months of management fees for a total owed of $165,000. However, Plaintiff made no attempt to explain how it reached an estimated amount of $100,000 in attorneys' fees, providing no estimate of necessary hours or the hourly rate to be charged. As such, Plaintiff has failed to satisfy its burden of showing that this amount equals the reasonable attorney fees that will be incurred. Third, Shabahangi's declaration and the accompanying documentary evidence establish the probable validity of Plaintiff's claim that Defendant breached the Management Agreement. Finally, there is nothing in the record to suggest that Plaintiff, in filing the instant application for a writ of attachment, has any purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based. Thus, the application is granted for the issuance of a writ of attachment in the amount of $165,510.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
